purpose: User-invocable router skill for dashboard monitoring/operation commands. Maps !status, !logs, !inspect, !deps, !history, !cancel, !cancel-safe, !reset, !start, !stop, !guide, !update_dashboard, !export to their _commands/Synapse/*.md spec files. Also documents dashboard resolution rules (use SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID or DASHBOARD ID directive — never scan or auto-detect), dashboard ID formats (ide, 6-hex, dashboardN legacy), and the key principle that dashboard stats are derived from progress files (master maintains no counters). Includes shell preprocessing (`!\`...\``) that injects TRACKER_ROOT and DASHBOARDS list into the prompt at load time.
gotchas: Dispatcher skill — does NOT contain the actual command logic; that lives in _commands/Synapse/*.md
Dashboard resolution is strict: use assigned dashboard, never scan
Shell preprocessing runs at skill-load time, injecting live state into the prompt
argument-hint is `<command> [args]` — user types the command name as argument
patterns: Router skill: routing table + shell preprocessing + 'read the spec file' instruction
user-invocable: true — directly callable as a slash command
conventions: Shell blocks use !` ... ` syntax with backticks
